🤖 AI Data Prep Tools

Extract people from text/CSV, enrich ministerial diaries, and prepare mapping columns for Admin → Add Data

← Back to Dashboard
ℹ️

Which tool should I use?

Guided Picker
Are you importing this CSV via Admin → Add Data?
Is this a ministerial diary (meetings) and you need per-row attendees?
Capability CSV AI Preprocessor Name Finder (Diaries) Name Finder (Generic)
Output ai_* columns per row attendees_names per row deduped people list (aggregate)
Per-row vs Aggregate Per-row Per-row Aggregate
First/Last split Yes (ai_first_name/ai_last_name) No No
Org detection Yes (ai_org_name / ai_orgs) No No
Best for Admin import mapping Diaries → Meetings importer Discovery / QA

The two main tools here solve different but related problems around extracting and preparing people data for import:

When to choose which

  • You're importing arbitrary CSVs into people/parties/electorates/donations/meetings via Admin: use CSV AI Preprocessor first to get ai_* columns, then map those columns in the Admin importer.
  • You just want to know "who is in here?" across a document: use Name Finder (Generic).
  • You're prepping diaries for the meetings importer: use Name Finder (Diaries) for attendees_names per row. If you later need first/last split for specific tables, run CSV AI Preprocessor to get ai_first_name/ai_last_name.

Optional improvements

  • CSV AI Preprocessor: add a note “Use this before Admin → Add Data.”
  • In Diaries results, you can also download enriched CSV (includes attendees_names); optionally add ai_first_name/ai_last_name as hints per row.
  • Add a note under Name Finder (Generic) that it's not meant for per-row import; use CSV AI Preprocessor for Admin imports.

Conclusion: Name Finder is great for discovery and diaries row-level people lists. CSV AI Preprocessor creates structured ai_* columns for fast, accurate Admin importing. They solve adjacent but different workflows, so both are useful.

📄

Name Finder

Mode:
In Diaries mode, upload a CSV with headers: Minister, Date or Date Started, Date Finished, Schedule Time, Type, Meeting, Location, With, Portfolio.
When enabled, the whole file is chunked and sent to Gemini to extract people names. This can be slower and use more tokens, but may find more names in messy files.

Drop your file here or click to browse

Supported formats: TXT, CSV, DOC, DOCX, PDF (max 1MB)

Selected file:

Size:

🧹

CSV AI Preprocessor (adds mapping columns)

Use this to clean and standardize any CSV before importing via Admin → Add Data.
It adds AI helper columns to assist matching and mapping:
ai_first_name, ai_last_name, ai_org_name, ai_person_names, ai_orgs
Use this before Admin → Add Data.

Upload any CSV to prepare for Admin → Add Data

AI will add helper columns: ai_first_name, ai_last_name, ai_org_name, ai_person_names, ai_orgs

Upload a CSV and click "Prepare Mapping CSV" to see the preview and download options.
